title: veronica ciliolata subsp. ciliolata
intro: Veronica ciliolata subsp. ciliolata, also known as the lesser marsh speedwell, is a perennial plant species belonging to the plantain family (Plantaginaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, to leverage its medicinal properties.
